commands.review_summary:
Adds an Ollama stream cooperative scheduler and abort checks while draining native NDJSON chunks, plus a dense-chunk timer-yield regression test.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +41. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the scoped dense native Ollama stream path: current main has no bounded yield in the chunk loop, and the PR adds a focused timer-yield regression. No for the full linked Windows local-daemon report, which the PR explicitly leaves open.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the maintainer-labeled member PR needs a human landing decision and should not close the broader linked issue by itself.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- adds a regression test that proves timers can run before dense stream processing completes

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate against the rebased maintainer branch, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, formatter/diff checks, and branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` with 11/11 tests after rebase at `e80a8b670fd`; oxfmt and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_44ae10129cdd`, slug `golden-prawn`, run `run_565207623b61`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions` and `extensionTests`.

Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
target.author: vincentkoc
